pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI/ML Engineering falls outside the primary competency scope of the VP Data Governance. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: Seniority fit was marked 'too_junior', but the dimension is Knowledge — awareness-level KSAs are typically appropriate across seniority tiers.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Even Knowledge-level KSAs that describe specific low-level technical details (e.g., implementation internals) are not expected at the vp level. Exclusion upheld.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: Exclusion upheld: Knowledge KSAs with predominantly execution-oriented framing are appropriately excluded at the vp tier.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is AI/ML Engineering truly out-of-scope for the VP Data Governance? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list AI/ML Engineering as a core or recommended competency for the VP Data Governance. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms AI/ML Engineering falls outside the established competency profile for the VP Data Governance. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: AI/ML Engineering is outside the competency scope of the VP Data Governance. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
